title: Best Web Scraping Tools in 2026: 8 Platforms Compared
desc: The best web scraping tools in 2026 compared: Apify, Bright Data, Octoparse, Firecrawl and more, with real pricing, honest pros and cons, and who each is for.
keywords: best web scraping tools, best web scraping tools 2026, web scraping software, web scraping tools comparison, no-code web scraping, web scraping api, apify alternatives, best scraping tools for lead generation
cta: Want scraped, enriched leads without building the pipeline?
body:
Quick answer: for most people the best web scraping tool in 2026 is Apify, because its marketplace of more than 36,000 ready made scrapers means you rarely have to write any code, and the free plan gives you $5 of monthly usage with no credit card so you can test before you pay. If you need enterprise scale and the hardest anti bot sites, Bright Data wins. If you are not a developer, Octoparse is the friendliest. If you are feeding an AI model or agent, Firecrawl returns clean, ready to read data. Below we compare the eight tools we actually use and test, with real 2026 pricing and the honest catch with each one.

Web scraping tools at a glance

Web scraping tools fall into three groups in 2026: no code platforms you point and click, marketplaces and APIs that handle the hard parts for you, and open source libraries you run yourself. Here is the short version of who each one is for and where pricing starts.

  • Apify: best all around. More than 36,000 ready made scrapers plus a real free tier. From $0, then $29 per month.
  • Bright Data: best for enterprise scale and tough, protected sites. Pay as you go from about $1.50 per 1,000 records, plans from $499 per month.
  • Octoparse: best no code desktop tool for non developers. Free plan, then $69 per month.
  • Firecrawl: best for AI and LLM ready output. Free 500 credits, then $16 per month.
  • ScraperAPI: best simple API for high volume. Free 1,000 credits a month, then $49 per month.
  • ScrapingBee: best lightweight API for small dev teams. From $49 per month.
  • Browse AI: best no code monitoring and change alerts. Free tier, then about $19 per month billed annually.
  • Crawlee: best free open source library for developers who want full control. Free.

1. Apify, best all around web scraping platform

Best for: almost everyone, from solo founders to data teams, especially if you would rather run a proven scraper than build one. Apify is a cloud platform whose Apify Store holds more than 36,000 prebuilt scrapers, called Actors, for Google Maps, LinkedIn, Instagram, TikTok, Amazon, and thousands of other sites. You can be pulling structured data within minutes of signing up, with no code.

Pricing (verified June 2026): Free at $0 with $5 of monthly usage and no credit card, Starter at $29 per month, Scale at $199 per month, and Business at $999 per month, with 10 percent off on annual billing. Cost is usage based, billed in compute units (1 GB of RAM for one hour) at $0.20 per unit on the lower plans down to $0.13 on Business. Students and startups can get 30 percent off.

The catch: because pricing is usage based, your bill is hard to predict until you run a test, and unused monthly credits do not roll over. Heavy users also pay for proxies and data transfer on top.

For most readers it is the safest first stop. Start free on Apify and run a test scrape before you commit to a paid plan.

2. Bright Data, best for enterprise scale and tough sites

Best for: large teams scraping millions of pages, or sites with aggressive anti bot defenses. Bright Data runs one of the largest proxy networks in the world and pairs it with a Web Scraper API, a Web Unlocker, and a SERP API.

Pricing: the Web Scraper API is pay as you go from about $1.50 per 1,000 records, with monthly plans starting around $499 for roughly half a million records, and proxies from about $0.90 per IP. Volume buyers negotiate 20 to 40 percent off.

The catch: it is built for scale, so the pricing, dashboard, and product sprawl can overwhelm a small team, and costs climb quickly. It is overkill if you only need a few thousand records a month.

3. Octoparse, best no code tool for non developers

Best for: people who want to scrape without writing code or thinking about proxies. Octoparse is a visual, point and click desktop app for Windows and Mac, with more than 500 ready made templates, built in IP rotation, and automatic CAPTCHA solving on paid tiers.

Pricing: a free forever plan covers 10 tasks and 50,000 rows of export a month. Standard is $69 per month and Professional is $249 per month on annual billing, adding cloud extraction and more concurrent tasks.

The catch: the desktop app has a learning curve of its own, cloud runs are limited on lower tiers, and very complex or heavily protected sites can still trip it up.

4. Firecrawl, best for AI and LLM ready data

Best for: developers and AI teams who want a page turned into clean markdown that a language model can read. Firecrawl handles JavaScript, CAPTCHAs, and anti bot detection, then returns tidy, structured output built for retrieval and agents.

Pricing: a free tier of 500 credits to start, Hobby at $16 per month for 3,000 credits, and Growth at $333 per month for 500,000 credits. Most endpoints cost one credit per page.

The catch: it is subscription only with no pay as you go, credits expire monthly, and the free 500 credits are a one time allowance rather than a monthly refill. It is focused on AI use cases more than general purpose scraping at huge scale.

5. ScraperAPI, best simple API for high volume

Best for: developers who just want one API endpoint that handles proxies, browsers, and CAPTCHAs, then returns the HTML. You send a URL, you get the page back.

Pricing: free with 1,000 credits a month, Hobby at $49 per month for 100,000 credits, Startup at $149 for one million, Business at $299 for three million, and Scaling at $475 for five million. A request costs 1 to 75 credits depending on the site and the anti bot features needed.

The catch: credits do not carry over, and the credit cost per request can climb on hard sites, so your effective price per page is often higher than the headline rate suggests.

6. ScrapingBee, best lightweight API for small teams

Best for: small developer teams who want a clean, well documented API and headless browser rendering without managing any infrastructure.

Pricing: plans start at $49 per month for the Freelance tier, $149 for Startup, and $599 for Business, with about 20 percent off on annual billing. As with most API tools, a request costs from 1 credit for plain HTML up to 75 for stealth proxies.

The catch: the per credit model means JavaScript and premium proxy requests eat credits fast, so a plan that looks generous can run out sooner than expected on protected sites.

7. Browse AI, best no code monitoring and alerts

Best for: non technical users who want to watch specific pages and get alerted when something changes, like a competitor price or a job listing. You train a robot by clicking through a page once.

Pricing: a free plan with 50 credits a month, then paid plans from about $19 per month billed annually, scaling up through Professional and Team tiers. A credit is spent each time a robot runs.

The catch: credits burn quickly if you monitor many pages often, and it is built more for monitoring and small extractions than for scraping millions of rows.

8. Crawlee, best free open source library

Best for: developers who want full control and no platform fees, and are happy to host and maintain their own scrapers. Crawlee is a free, open source Node.js and Python library, maintained by Apify, that handles queues, retries, and anti blocking patterns for you.

Pricing: free. You only pay for your own servers and any proxies you add.

The catch: you build and maintain everything yourself, including proxy rotation and scaling. There is no dashboard and no support line, so it costs engineering time instead of subscription dollars.

How to choose the right web scraping tool

Match the tool to who is using it. If you are not a developer, start with Octoparse or Browse AI. If you are a developer who wants speed, a marketplace like Apify or a simple API like ScraperAPI or ScrapingBee will save you days. If you are feeding an AI model, Firecrawl returns the cleanest output. If you are scraping at enterprise scale or hitting walls on protected sites, Bright Data is built for it. And if you want zero platform cost and have engineers, Crawlee is free.

Whatever you pick, always test on a small run first so you can see the real per page cost, and budget for proxies on harder sites. If you would rather skip the tooling entirely, our team can build and run the whole pipeline for you as part of our AI automation and lead generation services.

From scraped data to booked meetings

Scraping is only step one. The real value shows up when clean data flows into a system that turns it into revenue: enrich the records, verify the emails, then run a disciplined outreach sequence. That is exactly the workflow we build for clients. Scrape with a tool like Apify, enrich and verify, then send through a deliverability safe cold email system.

If your goal is specifically B2B contact data rather than general web data, see our guide to the best B2B lead scraping tools, which ranks the contact databases by accuracy. For a deeper look at the platform we recommend most, read our full Apify review. When you are ready, you can create a free Apify account and have your first dataset within the hour.
faqs: What is the best web scraping tool in 2026?

For most people it is Apify, because its marketplace of more than 36,000 prebuilt scrapers means you rarely write code, and the free plan lets you test with no credit card. Developers working at enterprise scale often prefer Bright Data, and non developers tend to like the point and click simplicity of Octoparse.
What is the best free web scraping tool?

Apify and Octoparse both have genuine free tiers, Apify with $5 of monthly usage and Octoparse with 10 tasks and 50,000 rows a month. For developers, Crawlee is fully open source and free to self host, though you still pay for your own servers and any proxies you use.

Scraping publicly available data is generally legal in many regions, but it depends on the website's terms, the type of data, and local laws such as the GDPR. Avoid scraping personal or copyrighted data without a lawful basis, respect robots rules and rate limits, and seek advice for anything sensitive. This is general information, not legal advice.
Which web scraping tool is best for AI and LLMs?

Firecrawl is purpose built for this, returning clean markdown that a language model can read directly. Apify also runs an MCP server, so AI agents can trigger its scrapers, which makes it a strong choice for agent based workflows in 2026.
How much do web scraping tools cost in 2026?

Entry paid plans range from about $16 a month for Firecrawl Hobby to $49 for ScraperAPI and ScrapingBee and $69 for Octoparse, while Apify starts at $29 and keeps a free tier. Enterprise platforms like Bright Data start around $499 a month. Most tools bill by credits or usage, so run a small test first to learn your real cost.
Do I need to know how to code to scrape websites?

No. No code tools like Octoparse and Browse AI let you scrape by pointing and clicking, and Apify's marketplace runs prebuilt scrapers for you. Coding only becomes necessary when you need fully custom logic, which is where a library like Crawlee or an API like ScraperAPI helps.
